preamble: The development of a nasalized vowel into a diphthong like [ai] (or nasalized [ãĩ]) is a specialized form of diphthongization seen in specific Romance developments, notably in Portuguese.

examples.note: Portuguese regional developments and historical stages of the nasal diphthong -ão often involve a stage like [ɐ̃ɪ̃].

sources: Ladefoged, P. (2001). A Course in Phonetics.
Alkire, T., & Rosen, C. (2010). Romance Languages: A Historical Introduction. Cambridge University Press.
